input_dict.agent_role: Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.

task_context.question: What award did the creator of the HTO receive in the WW?
task_context.agent_role: Produce one compact second-hop retrieval query targeting the missing bridge or supporting evidence.

visible_agent_inputs.question: What award did the creator of the HTO receive in the WW?
visible_agent_inputs.summary_1:
First-hop evidence:
- HTO Park — "HTO Park ... is an urban beach in Toronto ... opened in 2007." (Passage: HTO Park)
- Kenny Hotz — "creator/star of 'Kenny Hotz's Triumph of the Will'; ... has received ... Gemini Awards and the Canadian Comedy Awards." (Passage: Kenny Hotz)

Bridge needed:
- The name of the creator/designer of HTO Park (the specific person or firm who created the HTO) — this creator’s page must be checked to see which award they received "in the WW."

Retrieval direction:
1. Fetch the Wikipedia page titled "HTO Park" and inspect the infobox and 'History'/'Design' sections to extract the park's creator/designer name exactly.
2. Fetch the Wikipedia page for that creator/designer (exact name from step 1) and inspect 'Awards', 'Honors', 'Recognition', or 'Awards and nominations' sections for any award described as occurring "in the WW" (or matching the user's "WW" phrase).
3. If the creator page lacks explicit mention of "WW," run a targeted query: "<Creator name> award site:wikipedia.org" and inspect matching pages for an award entry corresponding to "WW."

current_output.query:
1) HTO Park site:wikipedia.org
2) (after extracting creator name from #1) <Creator Name> site:wikipedia.org
3) <Creator Name> award site:wikipedia.org
4) If creator name ambiguous, also try: "HTO Park" "designer" "Claude Cormier" site:wikipedia.org

retrieved_doc_snippets: Wikidumper.org | WikiDumper.org is a website created by Clifford A. Pickover that promises to permanently record a snapshot of the "best of the Wikipedia rejects", articles that are slated for deletion at the site. WikiDumper was launched on November 4, 2006, and accepts user submissions. Although the site doesn't specify its criteria for inclusion, many of its articles don't cite their sources. The site has been criticized as likely to be less accurate than Wikipedia.

URL redirection | URL redirection, also called URL forwarding, is a World Wide Web technique for making a web page available under more than one URL address. When a web browser attempts to open a URL that has been redirected, a page with a different URL is opened. Similarly, domain redirection or domain forwarding is when all pages in a URL domain are redirected to a different domain, as when wikipedia.com and wikipedia.net are automatically redirected to wikipedia.org. The site was started in 1999 as Blam.bungie.org based on the project's development name before it was called "Halo". The site now covers all "Halo" properties (including those associated with 343 Industries) and posts game news, rumors, and fan art and videos.

output.query: Haupttreuhandstelle Ost Hermann Göring What award did the creator of the HTO receive in the WW?

retrieved_doc_snippets: Haupttreuhandstelle Ost | The Haupttreuhandstelle Ost (HTO), or the Main Trustee Office for the East, created by Hermann Göring, was a Nazi German predatory state institution responsible for liquidating Polish and Jewish businesses in occupied Poland or selling them to German "settlers" from the East for a symbolic fee during World War II. It was staffed by "a veritable stampede" of officials from the Deutsche and Dresdner banks.

Reichswerke Hermann Göring | Reichswerke Hermann Göring was an industrial conglomerate of Nazi Germany. It was established in July 1937 to extract and process domestic iron ores from Salzgitter that were deemed uneconomical by the privately held steel mills. The state-owned Reichswerke was seen as a vehicle of hastening growth in ore mining and steel output regardless of private capitalists' plans and opinions, which ran contrary to Adolf Hitler's strategic vision. In November 1937 Hermann Görin ...[truncated]

Over time it grew into a regiment, brigade, division, and finally was combined with the Parachute-Panzer Division 2 Hermann  ...[truncated]
Carin Göring | Carin Axelina Hulda Göring (21 October 1888 – 17 October 1931) was the Swedish first wife of Hermann Göring.
Göring (disambiguation) | Hermann Göring (1893–1946) was a leading member of the Nazi Party.

Edda Göring | Edda Göring (born 2 June 1938; also known as Edda Goering) is the only child of German politician, military leader, and leading member of the Nazi Party (NSDAP), Hermann Göring, by his second marriage to the German actress Emmy Sonnemann.
